title: benstonea epiphytica
intro: Benstonea epiphytica, also known as the 'Wax Flower', is a species of flowering plant in the family Pandanaceae, native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, primarily in its roots, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The roots of Benstonea epiphytica have been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including wound healing, antimicrobial activities, and immune system support. In traditional herbal preparations, the roots of Benstonea epiphytica are often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
